outreach_subject: A European note from a longtime ArchDaily reader

replies.subject: Re: A European note from a longtime ArchDaily reader
replies.body:
Hi Lisa,

Thank you for your kind words about our projects coverage, and for
following ArchDaily closely. We really appreciate it.
We'll take a look at the material on My Villa and share it with the
relevant team internally. If it's a good fit for our editorial line, we'll
be in touch.

Best regards,

On Fri, Jul 10, 2026 at 3:19 AM Lisa Monelli <info@myvilla.la> wrote:

> Hi ArchDaily team,
>
> (Writing to the ArchDaily newsroom directly — this is meant for
> whichever desk is closest to the topic below. Feel free to forward as
> appropriate.)
>
> We follow the ArchDaily projects feed closely, it is one of the few
> places where residential material choices get shown alongside serious
> institutional work, which quietly raises the bar for how homes get
> talked about.
>
> A quick hello from our side: at My Villa, we bring European
> construction resilience and the Italian way of living to homes in Los
> Angeles, designed in exposed reinforced concrete, cemento a vista.
>
> One thought worth exploring: what LA is now codifying for wildfire
> resilience has been default Mediterranean building practice for
> generations. That cultural continuity feels like a residential story
> worth telling.
>
> Would you be interested in receiving more material on what we are
> doing, or in setting up a short call with our founder Paolo?
>
> Best,
> Lisa Monelli
> My Villa Media Team
> info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la
>
